file.content:
Settings

<%= form_with model: @setting, url: admin_settings_path, method: :patch, class: "space-y-8" do |form| %> <%# === GitHub OAuth === %>

GitHub OAuth

Allow users to sign in with their GitHub account.

<%= form.label :github_client_id, "Client ID", class: "block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700 mb-1" %> <%= form.text_field :github_client_id, value: @setting.github_client_id, placeholder: ENV["GITHUB_CLIENT_ID"].present? ? "Set via ENV" : "Enter client ID", class: "block w-full rounded-md border border-gray-300 px-3 py-2 text-sm focus:outline-blue-600" %>
<%= form.label :github_client_secret, "Client Secret", class: "block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700 mb-1" %> <%= form.password_field :github_client_secret, placeholder: @setting.github_client_secret.present? ? "Secret is set (leave blank to keep)" : "Enter client secret", class: "block w-full rounded-md border border-gray-300 px-3 py-2 text-sm focus:outline-blue-600" %>
<% if Setting.github_configured? %>

GitHub OAuth is active.

<% elsif ENV["GITHUB_CLIENT_ID"].present? %>

Currently using ENV variables.

<% end %>
<%# === Google OAuth === %>

Google OAuth

Allow users to sign in with their Google account.

<%= form.label :google_client_id, "Client ID", class: "block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700 mb-1" %> <%= form.text_field :google_client_id, value: @setting.google_client_id, placeholder: ENV["GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID"].present? ? "Set via ENV" : "Enter client ID", class: "block w-full rounded-md border border-gray-300 px-3 py-2 text-sm focus:outline-blue-600" %>
<%= form.label :google_client_secret, "Client Secret", class: "block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700 mb-1" %> <%= form.password_field :google_client_secret, placeholder: @setting.google_client_secret.present? ? "Secret is set (leave blank to keep)" : "Enter client secret", class: "block w-full rounded-md border border-gray-300 px-3 py-2 text-sm focus:outline-blue-600" %>
<% if Setting.google_configured? %>

Google OAuth is active.

<% elsif ENV["GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID"].present? %>

Currently using ENV variables.

<% end %>
<%# === SMTP === %>

SMTP / Email

Configure outgoing email for password resets and user invites.

<%= form.label :smtp_address, "SMTP Server", class: "block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700 mb-1" %> <%= form.text_field :smtp_address, placeholder: "smtp.example.com", class: "block w-full rounded-md border border-gray-300 px-3 py-2 text-sm focus:outline-blue-600" %>
<%= form.label :smtp_port, "Port", class: "block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700 mb-1" %> <%= form.number_field :smtp_port, class: "block w-full rounded-md border border-gray-300 px-3 py-2 text-sm focus:outline-blue-600" %>
<%= form.label :smtp_username, "Username", class: "block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700 mb-1" %> <%= form.text_field :smtp_username, placeholder: "user@example.com", class: "block w-full rounded-md border border-gray-300 px-3 py-2 text-sm focus:outline-blue-600" %>
<%= form.label :smtp_password, "Password", class: "block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700 mb-1" %> <%= form.password_field :smtp_password, placeholder: @setting.smtp_password.present? ? "Password is set (leave blank to keep)" : "Enter password", class: "block w-full rounded-md border border-gray-300 px-3 py-2 text-sm focus:outline-blue-600" %>
<%= form.label :smtp_authentication, "Authentication", class: "block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700 mb-1" %> <%= form.select :smtp_authentication, [["Plain", "plain"], ["Login", "login"], ["CRAM-MD5", "cram_md5"]], {}, class: "block w-full rounded-md border border-gray-300 px-3 py-2 text-sm focus:outline-blue-600" %>
<%= form.label :smtp_from_address, "From Address", class: "block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700 mb-1" %> <%= form.email_field :smtp_from_address, placeholder: "noreply@example.com", class: "block w-full rounded-md border border-gray-300 px-3 py-2 text-sm focus:outline-blue-600" %>
<%= form.label :smtp_starttls, class: "inline-flex items-center gap-2 text-sm text-gray-700 cursor-pointer" do %> <%= form.check_box :smtp_starttls, class: "rounded border-gray-300" %> Enable STARTTLS <% end %>
<% if Setting.smtp_configured? %>

SMTP is configured.

<% end %>
<%= form.submit "Save Settings", class: "rounded-md px-3.5 py-2.5 bg-blue-600 hover:bg-blue-500 text-white text-sm font-medium cursor-pointer" %>
<% end %>

file.content:
class Setting < ApplicationRecord
  encrypts :github_client_secret, :google_client_secret, :smtp_password

  def self.instance
    find_or_create_by(id: 1)
  end

  # --- Class-level accessors with ENV fallback ---

  %i[github_client_id google_client_id].each do |attr|
    define_singleton_method(attr) do
      val = instance.send(attr)
      val.presence || ENV[attr.to_s.upcase]
    end
  end

  %i[github_client_secret google_client_secret].each do |attr|
    define_singleton_method(attr) do
      val = instance.send(attr)
      val.presence || ENV[attr.to_s.upcase]
    end
  end

  def self.github_configured?
    github_client_id.present? && github_client_secret.present?
  end

  def self.google_configured?
    google_client_id.present? && google_client_secret.present?
  end

  def self.smtp_configured?
    instance.smtp_address.present?
  end

  def self.smtp_from_address
    val = instance.smtp_from_address
    val.presence || ENV["SMTP_FROM_ADDRESS"] || "noreply@example.com"
  end

  # Skip blank secret values so "leave blank to keep" works
  %i[github_client_secret google_client_secret smtp_password].each do |attr|
    define_method(:"#{attr}=") do |value|
      super(value) if value.present?
    end
  end

  def smtp_settings
    return {} unless smtp_address.present?

    settings = {
      address: smtp_address,
      port: smtp_port || 587,
      user_name: smtp_username.presence,
      password: smtp_password.presence,
      authentication: smtp_authentication.presence&.to_sym,
      enable_starttls_auto: smtp_starttls
    }
    settings.compact
  end
end

content.thinking:
The issue is clear. In the view, on line 88:
```erb
placeholder: @setting.smtp_password.present? ? "Password is set (leave blank to keep)" : "Enter password",
```

This calls `@setting.smtp_password` which triggers decryption. If the encrypted value in the database was encrypted with a different key (e.g., after a key rotation or redeployment), the decryption will fail with `ActiveRecord::Encryption::Errors::Decryption`.

Similarly on lines 22 and 50:
- `@setting.github_client_secret.present?`


- `@setting.google_client_secret.present?`

These also decrypt and could fail. The cleanest approach is to add helper methods in the Setting model that safely check if encrypted fields are set by rescuing decryption errors and treating them as if the value exists.

Writing helper methods... I'm mapping out all the places where decryption could fail—the view checks for `present?` on encrypted fields, the class-level accessor methods, and the `smtp_settings` method. Since the encryption keys likely changed or data got corrupted, I need to either clear the corrupted values in production or add rescue blocks around all these decryption calls to handle the errors gracefully. the corrupted encrypted data and fix the encryption keys. The error "AEAD authentication tag verification failed" points to a key mismatch—likely because the encryption configuration was just fixed in the previous commit, but the existing data was encrypted with the old key derivation format.

For an immediate livepatch, I should make the views resilient to decryption failures by adding safe accessor methods to the model that gracefully handle decryption errors, then update the views to use those methods instead of accessing the encrypted fields directly. Let me check the ActiveRecord::Encryption initializer to see exactly what changed.

     7→    deterministic_key:   ENV["AR_ENCRYPTION_DETERMINISTIC_KEY"],

file.content:
# Configure ActiveRecord Encryption from environment variables.
# Keys are generated once per installation by the installer and stored
# in $SANDCASTLE_HOME/.env, ensuring each deployment has unique keys.
if ENV["AR_ENCRYPTION_PRIMARY_KEY"].present?
  ActiveRecord::Encryption.configure(
    primary_key:         ENV["AR_ENCRYPTION_PRIMARY_KEY"],
    deterministic_key:   ENV["AR_ENCRYPTION_DETERMINISTIC_KEY"],
    key_derivation_salt: ENV["AR_ENCRYPTION_KEY_DERIVATION_SALT"]
  )
end

content.thinking:
The recent commit "fix(encryption): use keyword args for ActiveRecord::Encryption.configure" suggests that prior to this fix, the keys weren't being applied correctly. So any data that was encrypted with the old (broken) configuration is now unreadable.

The problem is:
1. Old code had wrong `configure` call - encryption was done with Rails' default keys (from `credentials.yml.enc`)
2. New code uses environment variable keys via `configure` with keyword args
3. Old encrypted data (using default/wrong keys) can't be decrypted with the new correct keys

The livepatch fix should:
1. Make the app resilient to decryption errors (rescue them gracefully)


2. Clear the corrupted data from the DB so it can be re-entered

For the livepatch, I'll add safe accessor methods in the `Setting` model that gracefully handle decryption failures, update the view to use these safe methods, then deploy both files to production. I'm considering adding predicate methods that rescue decryption errors and return a safe value indicating the field was encrypted but unreadable.

Comparing a few approaches: I could add individual predicate methods for each encrypted field, create a generic helper that returns nil on decryption failure, or override the encrypted attribute readers directly to handle errors transparently. Option C seems cleanest—overriding the readers to return nil on decryption errors means all existing code automatically becomes resilient without needing changes elsewhere. I need to be careful about how Active...

Still writing accessor methods... Record encrypted attribute - I can't just `define_method` to override it cleanly.

Actually, Option A is the cleanest for the view fix. Let me add `_set?` methods and update the view.

Wait, actually the problem is also in the class-level accessors which call `instance.send(attr)` which will decrypt. So those could also blow up in other places (like OAuth callbacks).

The most robust fix is to rescue at the encrypted attribute reader level. I'm overriding the reader methods for the three sensitive fields to catch decryption errors and return nil instead of raising.

This way the view checks like `@setting.smtp_password.present?` will work correctly, returning false when the password can't be decrypted so the placeholder text shows "Enter password" - which is the right behavior since the user needs to re-enter it anyway. The class-level accessors and `smtp_settings` method also benefit from this fix. Let me check the view for all the similar patterns on the other secrets.

content.text:
The issue is clear: the recent encryption fix (`use keyword args`) changed key derivation, so existing encrypted values in the DB are now unreadable. The view crashes when calling `.present?` on them.

Fix: rescue `Decryption` errors at the model reader level so nil is returned gracefully everywhere.

input.new_string:
# Return nil instead of raising on unreadable encrypted values (e.g. after key rotation)
  %i[github_client_secret google_client_secret smtp_password].each do |attr|
    define_method(attr) do
      super()
    rescue ActiveRecord::Encryption::Errors::Decryption
      nil
    end
  end

  # Skip blank secret values so "leave blank to keep" works
  %i[github_client_secret google_client_secret smtp_password].each do |attr|
    define_method(:"#{attr}=") do |value|
      super(value) if value.present?
    end
  end

class Setting < ApplicationRecord
  encrypts :github_client_secret, :google_client_secret, :smtp_password

  def self.instance
    find_or_create_by(id: 1)
  end

  # --- Class-level accessors with ENV fallback ---

  %i[github_client_id google_client_id].each do |attr|
    define_singleton_method(attr) do
      val = instance.send(attr)
      val.presence || ENV[attr.to_s.upcase]
    end
  end

  %i[github_client_secret google_client_secret].each do |attr|
    define_singleton_method(attr) do
      val = instance.send(attr)
      val.presence || ENV[attr.to_s.upcase]
    end
  end

  def self.github_configured?
    github_client_id.present? && github_client_secret.present?
  end

  def self.google_configured?
    google_client_id.present? && google_client_secret.present?
  end

  def self.smtp_configured?
    instance.smtp_address.present?
  end

  def self.smtp_from_address
    val = instance.smtp_from_address
    val.presence || ENV["SMTP_FROM_ADDRESS"] || "noreply@example.com"
  end

  # Skip blank secret values so "leave blank to keep" works
  %i[github_client_secret google_client_secret smtp_password].each do |attr|
    define_method(:"#{attr}=") do |value|
      super(value) if value.present?
    end
  end

  def smtp_settings
    return {} unless smtp_address.present?

    settings = {
      address: smtp_address,
      port: smtp_port || 587,
      user_name: smtp_username.presence,
      password: smtp_password.presence,
      authentication: smtp_authentication.presence&.to_sym,
      enable_starttls_auto: smtp_starttls
    }
    settings.compact
  end
end

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(settings): rescue Decryption errors on encrypted attribute readers

After the encryption key fix (keyword args), existing DB values encrypted
with the old implicit key can no longer be decrypted. Rescue
ActiveRecord::Encryption::Errors::Decryption in the model readers so
the settings page renders instead of 500-ing, and users can re-enter
the values.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

content.text:
Done. Here's what happened and what was fixed:

**Root cause:** The previous commit (`fix(encryption): use keyword args`) corrected how `AR_ENCRYPTION_PRIMARY_KEY` etc. are passed to `ActiveRecord::Encryption.configure`. But any secrets already stored in the DB were encrypted *before* that fix (with Rails' default key derivation instead of the env var keys), so they now fail AEAD tag verification on read.

**Fix:** Added rescue handlers directly on the model attribute readers for `github_client_secret`, `google_client_secret`, and `smtp_password` — they return `nil` instead of raising. This means:
- The settings page renders correctly (placeholder shows "Enter password")
- All `.present?` checks return false cleanly
- The class-level OAuth accessors also benefit
- Users can simply re-enter the secrets and they'll be saved with the correct key
